review_summary: The transcript and unit summary provide enough grounded detail to generate 2 reinforcement questions about the motivation for speech BCIs without needing a separate video clip.

question_bank.question: Why are letter boards and eye-tracking keyboards described as insufficient for many people with severe speech impairment?
choices: They are slow and physically tiring to use
They require internet access to work
They can be used only in hospitals
question_bank.explanation: These tools can help, but they still demand slow selection and sustained eye control, which can be exhausting for users with limited residual movement.

question_bank.question: What is the central goal of a speech-related brain-computer interface?
choices: Bypass impaired muscles and use brain signals directly for communication or device control
Train users to speak more slowly so errors decrease
Replace the brain with a language model
Improve eye-tracking calibration on virtual keyboards
question_bank.explanation: The motivating idea is to read signals from an intact brain directly so communication and control no longer depend entirely on damaged speech or motor pathways.
